title: Pagination View Models, Immutable State, Map Transitions, and Stale Results · Type Recall
summary: A delayed maintainer review reconstructs pagination view models, immutable state, map transitions, and stale results from revision, runtime, command, unknown input, validation result, request identity, response fixture, promise state, cache decision, output, abort, cleanup, and recovery evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for immutable stale-safe Pokemon page state after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, caus…
objectives: Convert validated transport pages into readonly resource, cursor, result identity, count, and source metadata.
Derive candidate navigation and commit only a validated successful page while preserving prior state on failure.
Assign operation identity or generation so late responses cannot overwrite current state after newer commands.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Separate evidence from confidence
steps.instruction:
review case — A review TypeScript team handles case 6886 during an inclusive museum learning kiosk. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to commit immutable validated pages while excluding failed and out-of-order stale results; browser TypeScript must remain deterministic and free of Node or network effects; then change one unknown JSON field, nested member, status, URL, or byte budget and re-run validation. Browser code uses pure bounded runtime models and original fixtures only. Real PokéAPI traffic, Node readline, host streams, signals, files, package installs, handle and load claims, accessibility review, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Script rapid next, back, abort, failure, short page, repeated view, and out-of-order responses and assert every state.
